symbolic_trained_ported_external_gt.note: test set has ZERO confirmed-negative examples -- precision=1.0 whenever n_flagged>0 is a tautology of the label design, not a real precision measurement; only recall (fraction of externally-confirmed TRUE pairs recovered) is informative here

test set has ZERO confirmed-negative examples -- precision=1.0 whenever n_flagged>0 is a tautology of the label design, not a real precision measurement; only recall (fraction of externally-confirmed TRUE pairs recovered) is informative here

verdict.headline: NEGATIVE RESULT (4th independent confirmation tonight): a learned classifier (logreg/shallow-tree/GBM) trained directly on REAL AUDIO via leave-one-song-out CV, with richer features (block_distance_norm, local_variance, abs_diff between audio/symbolic similarity), does NOT beat a plain audio_sim threshold at a near-zero-FPR operating point once the tautological symbolic_sim feature is removed.
verdict.circularity_caught: The apparent large win (grain=4, full feature set, logreg: recall=0.601 @ realized_fpr=0.011 vs threshold baseline recall=0.104 @ same fpr) is explained almost entirely by symbolic_sim/abs_diff being derived from the SAME baseline-decode bar buckets that pseudo_gt_match is defined from (mismatches<=1 of those same buckets) -- the exact circularity already flagged in known_issues.md for the bar-level joint gate ("agree and symbolic_sim are BOTH derived from the same baseline decode"). Audio-only ablation (drop symbolic_sim, abs_diff) collapses logreg recall from 0.601 to 0.018 and GBM from 0.828 to 0.055 at FPR<=0.01 -- both now WORSE than the plain threshold baseline (0.104).
verdict.grain_8_structural_finding: grain=8 has essentially zero positive pseudo_gt_match labels in the real-audio full census pooled across all 3 songs (1/1244 pairs) -- not a modeling problem, a label-scarcity problem; no classifier (linear or nonlinear) can be trained or validated on real audio at grain=8 with the current pipeline.
verdict.external_gt_design_gap: external_gt_match, as built (reusing the aretha-vamp-window and autumn_leaves A/A-repeat coverage rules from the prior real-audio validation call), only ever assigns label=1 or label=None -- it has ZERO confirmed-negative real-audio examples. This makes it unusable for FPR-gating or precision measurement in this call, and was previously reported with a vacuous precision=1.0 in an earlier internal draft of this run before being caught and explicitly flagged (see precision_is_vacuous field). A genuine external NEGATIVE real-audio corpus (comparable in scale to abbas one confirmed-FP bar pair, but at block grain) is the concrete missing ingredient, not a modeling gap.
verdict.relation_to_bar_level_finding: Directly replicates scratchpad/merge_criterion.py bar-level result (logreg lost to threshold at FPR<=0.05) at grain=4/8: once circular features are removed, plain threshold beats every learned model tested here too. More complex != better, confirmed a second time at a different grain.
verdict.recommendation: Do not ship a learned grain=4/8 auto-tier classifier. The bottleneck is NOT model class (tried linear, shallow tree, GBM) -- it is the absence of a real, non-circular, negatively-labeled real-audio dataset at block grain. Before any future learned-classifier attempt at this grain, the concrete missing prerequisite is: externally verify at least ~10-20 CONFIRMED-FALSE real-audio block pairs (not just confirmed-true) across at least 2 songs, by ear/chart cross-reference -- symmetric to what already exists for confirmed-true (aretha 11/11, autumn_leaves 1/1). Until that exists, stick to the already-shipped human-reviewed suggestion tier; the joint audio+symbolic gate (39.4%->89.6% pooled precision, bar-level) remains the best available compromise, itself not cleared for silent auto-apply.
